---
title: "Vendor Management: Add, Organize & Invite Vendors"
sidebarTitle: "Vendors"
description: "Add and manage your supply-side vendors — set payment terms, banking details, agreements, and invite them to the vendor portal."
---

Vendors are the suppliers you procure digital goods from, and every purchase order, offer, and procurement transaction in Tokensupply is tied to one. You manage the full vendor lifecycle — from first contact through signed agreements — directly inside the platform without needing external spreadsheets or contact managers.

## Finding your vendors

Navigate to **Purchases → Vendors** to open the vendor list. Each row shows the vendor's name, status (Active / Inactive), country, default currency, payment terms, and total number of purchase orders placed with them.

## Adding a vendor

Click **Add Vendor** in the top-right corner of the list to open the vendor form. The form is divided into five named sections — fill them in order.

<Steps>
  <Step title="Basic details">
    Enter the vendor's **Name**, then choose a **Vendor Type** — either **Individual** or **Business**. Select their **Country** and **Default Currency**, then set their **Status** to Active or Inactive.

    <Info>
      The vendor type you choose here controls how tax is calculated on every purchase order for this vendor. Choose carefully — it affects compliance.
    </Info>
  </Step>

  <Step title="Contact information">
    Enter the vendor's primary **Email**, **Phone**, and **Address**. You can also add chat handles for **Telegram**, **WhatsApp**, and **Instagram** if you coordinate with them on those platforms.
  </Step>

  <Step title="Catalogue">
    Select the categories of goods this vendor supplies. Available categories are:

    - Gift Cards
    - Gaming / Software Keys
    - Subscriptions
    - Licenses
    - In-game Currency
    - DLCs
    - Other
  </Step>

  <Step title="Commercial terms">
    Choose the vendor's default **Payment Terms**. This value is pre-filled on every new purchase order you create for them, though you can always override it per PO.

    <CardGroup cols={2}>
      <Card title="Standard net terms">
        Net 7, Net 15, Net 30, Net 45, Net 60, Net 90
      </Card>
      <Card title="Immediate terms">
        Due on Receipt, Cash on Delivery (COD), Advance
      </Card>
    </CardGroup>
  </Step>

  <Step title="Banking details">
    Enter the vendor's bank account details for payment. The fields shown adapt automatically to the vendor's country:

    | Country / Region | Fields shown |
    |---|---|
    | India | IFSC code, account number |
    | United States | Routing number, account number |
    | United Kingdom | Sort code, account number |
    | Australia | BSB, account number |
    | EU / international | IBAN, SWIFT/BIC |

    Additional payment identifiers such as UPI ID or PayPal email can also be added where applicable.
  </Step>
</Steps>

Save the form and Tokensupply assigns the vendor a sequential code in the format `SLR-001`, `SLR-002`, and so on. This code is used as the vendor prefix in every PO number (for example, `PO-SLR-001-0042`).

## Vendor types and tax treatment

The **Vendor Type** you set drives tax behaviour across the entire relationship.

<Tabs>
  <Tab title="Individual">
    Individual vendors are treated as unregistered sellers. All purchase orders raised for them carry **0% tax** — there is no override. The generated PO PDF includes a **Tax Declaration** notice in place of the standard tax breakdown, which serves as your compliance record.

    The vendor badge displays in **blue** on the vendor card and on every PO header.
  </Tab>
  <Tab title="Business">
    Business vendors receive automatic tax rates based on a comparison of your store's country and the vendor's country. For example, an Indian vendor supplying to an Indian store triggers **GST 18%**, while certain EU cross-border scenarios produce **0% reverse charge**.

    You can toggle **Tax Inclusive** on individual POs if the unit price the vendor quotes already includes tax.

    The vendor badge displays in **orange**.
  </Tab>
</Tabs>

## Vendor detail page

Click any vendor in the list to open their detail page. At the top you'll find a profile header and quick stats — total POs, total spend, and open tickets. Below that, seven tabs organise all vendor-related data:

<CardGroup cols={2}>
  <Card title="Overview" icon="layout-dashboard">
    A summary of the vendor's profile, contact details, banking info, and commercial terms.
  </Card>
  <Card title="Purchase Orders" icon="file-invoice">
    Every PO raised for this vendor, filterable and searchable without leaving the vendor page.
  </Card>
  <Card title="Products" icon="box">
    Products this vendor is mapped to through active or inactive offers.
  </Card>
  <Card title="Offers" icon="tag">
    All vendor offers — pricing bids for specific products — with the ability to add, edit, or deactivate them.
  </Card>
  <Card title="Agreements" icon="file-contract">
    Signed contracts and NDAs uploaded as PDF or DOCX files.
  </Card>
  <Card title="Tickets" icon="ticket">
    Support tickets that involve this vendor, scoped automatically.
  </Card>
  <Card title="Activity" icon="activity">
    A chronological audit trail of every change made to this vendor record.
  </Card>
</CardGroup>

## Inviting a vendor to the portal

The vendor portal gives your suppliers a secure, scoped workspace where they can submit stock, upload invoices, and manage their offers without requiring direct admin access to your store.

From the vendor detail page, click **Invite to Portal**. Tokensupply sends the vendor a setup email — delivered via your configured SMTP connection, with Resend used as an automatic fallback if SMTP is not set up.

The invitation card on the vendor detail page reflects the current state:

| State | Meaning | Available actions |
|---|---|---|
| **No portal access** | No invite has been sent | Click **Invite to Portal** to begin |
| **Pending Access** | Invite sent, vendor has not completed setup | **Resend invite** or **Revoke invite** |
| **Active** | Vendor has accepted and can sign in | **Revoke access** |

<Tip>
  Revoking and re-inviting is safe at any time. The new invite supersedes the previous one and the card immediately updates to **Pending Access**. The vendor's account is not deleted — only their membership for your store is removed.
</Tip>

## Managing agreements

The **Agreements** tab on the vendor detail page lets you upload signed contracts, NDAs, and any other legal documents associated with the vendor.

- Accepted file formats: **PDF** and **DOCX**
- Files are stored in a **private storage bucket** — they are never publicly accessible
- Download links use **short-lived signed URLs** that expire automatically

Upload a document by clicking **Upload Agreement** within the Agreements tab, then selecting your file.

## Editing and deleting vendors

To update any vendor detail, click **Edit** in the profile header. All fields from the original Add Vendor form are editable.

<Warning>
  Deleting a vendor is permanent and requires you to type **DELETE** to confirm. Linked purchase orders, vendor offers, and transactions are **unlinked** — they are not deleted, but they lose their association with the vendor record. Use Edit to deactivate a vendor instead of deleting them if you want to preserve the full history.
</Warning>
